Plan your safari
The lodge

A camp built to disappear into the bush

Sabela was built in 2020 on a dry riverbed our family has known for three generations.

We worked with a Cape Town architect and local craftspeople to set eight suites lightly into the landscape — rammed earth, weathered timber, glass that vanishes. Nothing taller than the tree line, nothing the bush can't reclaim. The idea was never to compete with the wild, only to give you the best seat in front of it.

2020
Lodge opened
8
Suites only
4.99
Guest rating
Your team in the bush

Themba Nkosi & the Sabela guides

Themba has tracked this reserve for eighteen years and leads our guiding team. With him are six rangers and trackers — most born within sight of the gate — who read the bush in a way no field guide can teach.

A maximum of six guests per vehicle means your sightings are unhurried, and your questions always have time to be answered.

Meet us at the airstrip
What we stand for

Three commitments behind every stay

01

Conservation

A share of every booking funds anti-poaching units and habitat restoration across 22,000 unfenced hectares.

02

Community

Sabela is majority locally staffed, and our trust funds schooling and a tracker apprenticeship in the neighbouring village.

03

Low impact

Solar-powered, off-grid water, and a build designed to leave the smallest possible mark on the riverbed.

Come see it for yourself

The reserve is at its most generous when you have nowhere else to be. Find your dates and book direct.

Check availability
SSabela

An intimate, design-led safari lodge on a private Big Five reserve in the Greater Kruger.
